title: Notes from the Treetops
synopsis: Bramble the young raccoon finds himself stranded high in the rainforest canopy when his branch breaks, and must find a way to communicate with Kiki, a toucan who speaks only in musical chirps. Through a series of hilarious mishaps involving fallen fruit, tangled vines, and upside-down acrobatics, they discover that friendship doesn't need words - just understanding and lots of laughter.

pages.text:
My branch just snapped and now I'm stuck on this tiny platform of twigs fifty feet up!

Dear Anyone Who Finds This Leaf,

I am Bramble. I live in the emerald canopy where the air tastes like sweet fruit and morning mist. The vines twist around tree trunks thicker than my whole family den. Everything sparkles with dewdrops up here.

But right now I have a HUGE problem. I was reaching for the juiciest mango ever when CRACK! My branch broke. Now I'm stranded on this wobbly platform of sticks that creaks every time I breathe.

I tried to climb down but the bark is too slippery. I tried to jump but the next branch is too far. I tried to call for help but my voice just echoes in the misty green forever.

So I'm writing this note on the biggest leaf I can find. Maybe someone will see it and help me get home.

If you find this, please come to the tall tree with the purple flowers. I'll be the raccoon doing a silly dance to get your attention.

Hopelessly yours,
Bramble

P.S. - I'm dropping this leaf now. Here goes nothing!

illustration.description: Bramble, a young raccoon with a black mask, fluffy grey-and-black ringed tail, small paws, and mischievous amber eyes, sits on a tiny platform of broken twigs high in the rainforest canopy. He's scratching symbols onto a large banana leaf with a stick, surrounded by emerald green leaves and dangling vines. Warm orange sunlight filters through the misty canopy.
illustration.alt: A raccoon writing on a banana leaf while perched precariously on broken branches

pages.text:
Tweet-tweet-whistle-chirp-trill!

Dear Funny-Masked Tree-Climber,

I am Kiki. I found your leaf stuck to my bright orange beak after flying through a spider web. (Very embarrassing!)

I can see you up there doing your wiggly dance. You look very silly hanging upside-down like that! But I don't understand your scratch-marks on this leaf.

I only speak in chirps and tweets and whistles. You seem to speak in... whatever those squiggly lines are.

But don't worry! I will help you. I am an excellent problem-solver. I once built a nest using only rubber tree sap and parrot feathers. (It was very bouncy.)

I'm going to try dropping you some vines. Watch out below!

Chirp-tweet-whistle-trill,
Kiki

P.S. - Why do you keep waving your paws like that? It's very entertaining but I don't know what it means!

illustration.description: Kiki, a bright toucan with a large orange beak, hovers near Bramble, a young raccoon with a black mask, fluffy grey-and-black ringed tail, small paws, and mischievous amber eyes. Bramble is hanging upside-down from a vine, gesticulating wildly while Kiki drops more tangled vines that are getting all twisted around both of them. The rainforest canopy glows with warm yellows and oranges.
illustration.alt: A toucan dropping tangled vines to a raccoon who is hanging upside-down and gesturing

chapters.title: Pictures Work Better! (Drawn with Mashed Berries)

pages.text:
Dear Chirpy Friend,

Your vines got all tangled up and somehow I ended up wrapped like a burrito! It took me an hour to wiggle free. My tail is still purple from all the squished berries.

I think I understand now. You speak in tweets and I speak in... well, whatever this is. But maybe we can draw pictures!

Look, I'm drawing myself as a circle with a mask. And you're a circle with a big triangle beak. See?

And here's my problem: I'm drawing me on the high branch, then me falling, then me stuck. Do you understand?

I'm also drawing you flying over with a vine. But maybe a STRAIGHT vine this time? The curly ones just made everything worse.

Also, why did you drop that coconut on my head? Did you think I was hungry? Because I wasn't, but it was delicious anyway.

Berry-stained but hopeful,
Bramble

P.S. - I'm throwing this note in your direction. Please don't let it hit you in the beak!

illustration.description: Bramble, a young raccoon with a black mask, fluffy grey-and-black ringed tail, small paws, and mischievous amber eyes, is covered in purple berry juice and holding up a leaf covered in simple drawings. Berry pulp and coconut pieces are scattered around his tiny platform. The warm yellow sunlight makes the berry stains look golden against his grey fur.
illustration.alt: A berry-stained raccoon holding up a leaf with simple drawings while coconut pieces fall around him

chapters.title: We Did It! (Celebrated with Fruit Salad)

pages.text:
Dear New Best Friend,

Your pictures made perfect sense! I understood that you needed help getting down from your tree prison.

So I gathered my whole flock - my cousins Pablo, Rosita, and Mango. We worked together to weave you a vine ladder. (Although Pablo kept getting distracted by shiny things and Rosita insisted on making it 'prettier' with flower decorations.)

But it worked! You climbed down safely, even if you did slip on the last three rungs and land in that pile of rotting fruit. (You smelled very interesting afterward.)

Now we meet every morning for breakfast in the fig tree. You bring the jokes, I bring the musical entertainment, and we all bring way too much fruit.

I still don't understand your scratch-language and you still don't speak fluent whistle-chirp. But somehow when you pat your belly and point at the mangoes, I know exactly what you mean!

Forever your friend in the treetops,
Kiki

P.S. - Thank you for showing me how to hang upside-down. The world looks completely different from that angle!

illustration.description: Bramble, a young raccoon with a black mask, fluffy grey-and-black ringed tail, small paws, and mischievous amber eyes, sits at a makeshift table made of large leaves with Kiki the toucan and her colorful flock. They're sharing a feast of tropical fruits in the golden morning light filtering through the canopy. Everyone is laughing as Bramble demonstrates hanging upside-down while eating a mango.
illustration.alt: A raccoon and several toucans sharing breakfast together in the treetops with tropical fruit scattered around

coverPrompt.description: Bramble, a young raccoon with a black mask, fluffy grey-and-black ringed tail, small paws, and mischievous amber eyes, dangles upside-down from a vine in the emerald rainforest canopy, holding a leaf with pictures drawn on it. Colorful fruit and flowers surround him in warm yellows and oranges, with steam rising from the humid forest below.
coverPrompt.alt: A young raccoon hanging upside-down in a lush green rainforest, holding a leaf with drawings

questions.question: Why does Bramble get stuck in the tree?
questions.explanation: Bramble was reaching for a mango when his branch snapped and broke.

questions.question: How do Bramble and Kiki talk to each other?
questions.explanation: Since they speak different languages, Bramble and Kiki communicate by drawing pictures on leaves.

questions.question: What happens when Kiki first tries to help with vines?
questions.explanation: Kiki's first vine rescue attempt goes wrong when the vines get tangled and wrap Bramble up like a burrito.

questions.question: Who helps Kiki make the vine ladder?
choices: Her cousins Pablo, Rosita, and Mango
questions.explanation: Kiki gets help from her cousins Pablo, Rosita, and Mango to weave a proper vine ladder.

questions.question: What do Bramble and Kiki do together at the end?
questions.explanation: Bramble and Kiki become friends and meet every morning for breakfast in the fig tree.
